title: hornungia procumbens subsp. procumbens
intro: Hornungia procumbens subsp. procumbens, also known as Hornungia procumbens, is a type of flowering plant in the Brassicaceae family, commonly found in Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues such as skin irritations and respiratory problems. Hornungia procumbens can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
